title: Trump Brokers 2-Week Iran Ceasefire — Strait of Hormuz to Reopen for Tankers
detail: President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran on the evening of April 7–8, reached less than two hours before his self-imposed 8 p.m. ET deadline. Trump had warned that 'a whole civilization will die tonight' if no agreement was reached. Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for safe tanker passage as the core condition; the US and Israel agreed to suspend bombing operations for the ceasefire period. Pakistan's prime minister facilitated negotiations and is scheduled to host continued diplomatic talks in Islamabad on April 10. The ceasefire marks the first direct US-Iran diplomatic de-escalation agreement in decades. Reports of isolated ceasefire violations in Gulf states emerged within hours of the announcement.

media.caption: Trump announces 2-week Iran ceasefire after warning of existential consequences for Iran

title: US Markets Stage Sharp Comeback on Iran Ceasefire Relief; S&P 500 +2.76%
detail: US equity markets staged a significant rebound on April 8 following the Iran ceasefire announcement and the associated drop in oil prices. The S&P 500 rose approximately 2.76%, with Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq 100 futures all up more than 2% in early trading. Speculative AI stocks led gains — Nvidia, Tesla, AMD, and Micron rose 4–10%. Delta Air Lines surged 12% after posting strong earnings. Oil prices fell sharply on reduced geopolitical risk premium, easing inflation concerns and supporting bond markets. The rally partially offset the 9–11% decline recorded during the prior week's tariff-driven selloff.

title: Artemis II Flight Day 8 — Crew on Final Return Leg; Splashdown Now April 10
detail: On April 8, the Artemis II crew was on Flight Day 8 of their return journey from the Moon. The crew conducted trajectory correction burns, tested Orion's attitude control systems, and participated in a ship-to-ship communication call with the ISS Expedition 74 crew. The planned splashdown date was refined to Friday, April 10, 2026 — one day earlier than the April 11 estimate — in the Pacific Ocean off San Diego. Crew members Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch (NASA), and Jeremy Hansen (CSA) continued in-flight procedures for radiation mitigation on the return trajectory.

media.caption: Artemis II Flight Day 8 — Crew on Final Return Leg; Splashdown Now April 10

title: Trump Imposes 50% Secondary Tariffs on Nations Supplying Iran with Weapons
detail: Alongside the ceasefire announcement, President Trump declared 50% secondary tariffs on any nation supplying Iran with weapons, effective immediately with no exclusions or exemptions. The measure extends Trump's secondary tariff doctrine beyond trade into foreign-policy enforcement, aiming to deter Iran from rearming during and after the ceasefire period. It applies to all arms-supplying countries regardless of existing trade agreements, and analysts noted potential flashpoints with China and Russia — the principal suppliers of Iranian weapons systems.

media.caption: Trump Imposes 50% Secondary Tariffs on Nations Supplying Iran with Weapons
